codeReview.summary: The feature adds age-based pruning in .factory/init.sh, but the deletion predicate is based on top-level directory mtime only, which can incorrectly classify still-active per-invocation derived-data directories as stale during long-running jobs.

issues.description: Pruning selects directories solely by `-not -newermt "${PRUNE_AGE_HOURS} hours ago"` and then deletes them with `rm -rf` (line 29). Active xcodebuild runs can continue working in nested paths without updating the top-level temp directory mtime, so a concurrent init after >4h can delete an in-use per-invocation directory. This violates the requirement to avoid deleting active run directories.

sharedStateObservations.observation: A new operational knob (`DERIVED_DATA_PRUNE_AGE_HOURS`) and startup pruning behavior were introduced, but shared-state guidance does not mention how validators should tune/disable pruning for unusually long-running runs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/init.sh:24-31 defines `DERIVED_DATA_PRUNE_AGE_HOURS`; .factory/library/user-testing.md derived-data isolation section documents mktemp isolation but no pruning/tuning guidance.

summary: Reviewed handoff, commit diff, transcript skeleton, and skill guidance for feature misc-prune-stale-derived-data-directories. Found one blocking correctness issue: age-only mtime pruning can remove active per-invocation derived-data directories in long-running/concurrent scenarios, so the feature does not fully satisfy the active-directory safety requirement.
